channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: US official accuses Belgian company of Chinese ties amid $10 billion tender competition
item.description: A US official has warned Marco Rubio of "harmful Chinese influence" as two Belgian companies compete for the largest dredging projects.

item.title: Belgium to continue €52 million tax fraud lawsuit against US investors
item.description: The US federal judge in New York has refused to dismiss Belgium's lawsuit against investors who allegedly set up sham pension funds to obtain tax refunds.

item.title: US says it's 'time to put its footprint back on Greenland'
item.description: Currently, the US has one military base in Greenland, compared to 17 installations during the Cold War.

item.title: France: Woman dies by suicide after jumping from 13th floor with three children
item.description: An investigation into "parental infanticide" has now been launched.

item.title: World still remains ill-prepared for pandemic, experts warn
item.description: According to the report, the measures introduced after Covid-19 are still insufficient to address the increasing risk of future pandemics.

item.title: Belgians on board of Gaza flotilla intercepted by Israeli navy
item.description: The Israeli navy began boarding the remaining ships of the Global Sumud Flotilla, a humanitarian convoy headed for Gaza, at approximately 09:50 on Monday.

item.title: Boeing confirms Chinese purchase of 200 planes
item.description: Boeing confirms that China plans to purchase 200 planes from it, following a similar announcement by Donald Trump.

item.title: Trump warns Taiwan against declaring independence
item.description: US President Donald Trump warns Taiwan, during a televised interview on Friday, against declaring independence from China.

item.title: U.S. Coast Guard reports $62-million drug bust off Colombia
item.description: US Coast Guard claims to have intercepted smugglers off the coast of Colombia, seizing over 3,700 kgs of illegal drugs worth the equivalent of €52.8 million.

item.title: Dozens dead in Central African Republic goldmine disaster
item.description: Dozens of people died on 6 May in a landslide at a gold mine in the west of the Central African Republic, local sources tell AFP.

item.title: US announces an extra $1.8 billion for UN humanitarian aid
item.description: The United States pledges an additional $1.8 billion for UN humanitarian aid in 2026, bringing the total to $3.8 billion.

item.title: Warsaw recognises same-sex marriages performed in the EU
Trump's 'Golden Dome' missile shield would cost $1,200 billion
item.description: Trump's proposed Golden Dome missile defence system would cost $1.2 trillion as against an initial estimate of $175 billion, the Congressional Budget Office says.

item.title: Small plane crashes in France, killing two
item.description: A light aircraft crashed on Monday in Fontainebleau, France, killing the pilot and passenger.

item.title: Trump to visit China this week
item.description: Beijing confirmed on Monday that the trip will mark the first visit by a sitting US president to China since 2017.

item.title: Qatari tanker becomes first ship to cross Strait of Hormuz
item.description: LNG shipments through the Strait of Hormuz show signs of revival as Qatar resumes exports on a key route previously blocked by regional tensions.

item.title: Oil firm Aramco reports huge net profits as prices surge
item.description: Brent crude hits $60 per barrel after Gulf military escalation; Aramco profits climb to new highs despite operational hurdles.

item.title: Eurovision festivities tarnished by widespread boycott
item.description: The 70th Eurovision Song Contest opens amid controversy and calls for political boycott. Austria braces for pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli demonstrations.

item.title: Hantavirus-affected ship reaches shores of Tenerife
item.description: The Hondius ship's outbreak of the lethal Andes hantavirus has triggered international medical evacuations in Spain, as fears mount over its deadly toll.

item.title: Hantavirus: Three more Belgians quarantined
item.description: Health teams monitor six exposed Belgians as cruise virus risks unfold — two remain abroad with repatriation imminent.
